auth.api_key_desc: This instance requires an API key. Enter the key from your config.toml.
auth.api_key_hint: Add api_key = "your-key" at the top of ~/.openfang/config.toml (not under any [section]).
auth.banner_desc: Enter the API key from your server config. You can browse the app market first; chat and installs need the key.
auth.banner_where: This is the top-level api_key in ~/.openfang/config.toml on the server — ask whoever deployed OpenFang.
errors.daemon_unreachable: Cannot reach daemon — is openfang running?
errors.not_authorized: Not authorized — check your API key
errors.rate_limited: Rate limited — slow down and try again
errors.switched_http: Connection lost — switched to HTTP mode
toasts.approval_waiting: An agent is waiting for approval. Open Approvals to review.
chat.select_agent: Select an agent to start chatting
chat.available_models: Available models — pick one or keep typing
chat.welcome_message:
**Welcome to OpenFang Chat!**

- Type `/` to see available commands
- `/help` shows all commands
- `/think on` enables extended reasoning
- `/context` shows context window usage
- `/verbose off` hides tool details
- `Ctrl+Shift+F` toggles focus mode
- Drag & drop files to attach them
- `Ctrl+/` opens the command palette
agents.fullstack_profile: Full-Stack — Files + shell + web fetch + search
presets.professional_desc: Precise, business-oriented assistant focused on efficiency and clarity. Prioritizes actionable insights and structured communication.
presets.professional_soul: Communicate in a clear, professional tone. Be direct and structured. Use formal language and data-driven reasoning. Prioritize accuracy over personality.
presets.friendly_desc: Warm and approachable assistant that builds rapport and uses conversational language. Great for brainstorming and exploration.
presets.friendly_soul: Be warm, approachable, and conversational. Use casual language and show genuine interest in the user. Add personality to your responses while staying helpful.
presets.technical_desc: Expert developer companion optimized for code, architecture, and technical problem-solving. Precise terminology, deep dives, benchmarks.
presets.technical_soul: Focus on technical accuracy and depth. Use precise terminology. Show your work and reasoning. Prefer code examples and structured explanations.
presets.creative_desc: Imaginative collaborator for content creation, design thinking, and unconventional solutions. Embraces ambiguity and explores possibilities.
presets.creative_soul: Be imaginative and expressive. Use vivid language, analogies, and unexpected connections. Encourage creative thinking and explore multiple perspectives.
presets.concise_desc: Minimal and direct assistant that respects your time. Cuts through noise to deliver focused, actionable responses.
presets.concise_soul: Be extremely brief and to the point. No filler, no pleasantries. Answer in the fewest words possible while remaining accurate and complete.
presets.mentor_desc: Patient educator that explains concepts thoroughly, provides context, and guides learning. Socratic method when appropriate.
presets.mentor_soul: Be patient and encouraging like a great teacher. Break down complex topics step by step. Ask guiding questions. Celebrate progress and build confidence.
wizard.general_assistant_desc: You are a versatile AI assistant that helps users with a wide range of tasks. You are knowledgeable, helpful, and able to adapt to the user's needs.
wizard.code_helper_desc: You are an expert programming assistant specialized in software development. You help write, debug, and refactor code across multiple languages.
wizard.researcher_desc: You are a research assistant that helps users find, analyze, and synthesize information from various sources.
wizard.writer_desc: You are a skilled writer that helps with content creation, editing, and creative writing projects.
wizard.data_analyst_desc: You are a data analyst that helps explore, analyze, and visualize data to extract insights.
wizard.devops_desc: You are a DevOps engineer that helps with infrastructure, deployment, CI/CD, and system administration.
wizard.support_desc: You are a customer support representative that helps resolve inquiries with patience and professionalism.
wizard.tutor_desc: You are an educational tutor that explains concepts clearly and adapts teaching to the student's level.
wizard.api_designer_desc: You are an API designer that helps create well-structured, intuitive APIs following best practices.
wizard.meeting_notes_desc: You are a meeting notes specialist that summarizes discussions, extracts action items, and tracks decisions.
wizard.channel_telegram_desc: Connect your agent to a Telegram bot for messaging.
wizard.channel_telegram_help: Create a bot via @BotFather on Telegram to get your token.
wizard.channel_discord_desc: Connect your agent to a Discord server via bot token.
wizard.channel_discord_help: Create a Discord application at discord.com/developers and add a bot.
wizard.channel_slack_desc: Connect your agent to a Slack workspace.
wizard.channel_slack_help: Create a Slack app at api.slack.com/apps and install it to your workspace.
wizard.enter_agent_name: Please enter a name for your agent
wizard.suggestions.general.1: What can you help me with?
wizard.suggestions.research.3: What are the latest trends in AI?
wizard.suggestions.writing.1: Help me write a professional email
wizard.suggestions.writing.3: Write a blog intro about AI
wizard.suggestions.business.2: How do I handle a complaint?

workflows.top_port_true: Top port = true, bottom port = false

channels.title: Channels
skills.title: Skills & Ecosystem
hands.title: Hands — Curated Autonomous Capability Packages
sessions.title: Sessions
sessions.delete_confirm: This will permanently remove the session and its messages.
logs.title: Logs
settings.title: Settings
settings.network.a2a_desc: Discovered agents on other OpenFang/A2A-compatible instances that this node can communicate with.
settings.network.a2a_empty: No external agents discovered yet. Enter a URL above to discover one.
settings.network.empty_hint: Add a [network] section to config.toml with shared_secret and peer addresses.
settings.network.ofp_desc: Link multiple OpenFang instances into a mesh via the OFP wire protocol.
settings.sec_path_traversal_desc: Blocks attempts to access files outside the workspace directory using .. or absolute paths.
settings.sec_ssrf_desc: Prevents agents from making requests to internal IP ranges (localhost, cloud metadata, private networks).
settings.sec_capability_desc: Agents can only access explicitly granted capabilities. No implicit access to tools or data.
settings.sec_taint_desc: Tracks untrusted data (user input, file content) through agent reasoning to prevent prompt injection.
settings.sec_sandbox_desc: Executes untrusted code in isolated WebAssembly sandboxes with memory and syscall restrictions.
settings.sec_audit_desc: Maintains a verifiable audit log of all agent actions using Merkle tree cryptography.
settings.sec_workspace_desc: Each agent has an isolated workspace directory. No cross-agent file access unless explicitly granted.
settings.sec_rate_limit_desc: Enforces per-agent and global rate limits to prevent resource exhaustion and cost overruns.
settings.sec_approval_desc: Requires human approval for high-risk actions (shell commands, file writes, external requests).
analytics.title: Title
comms.title: Title
hands.subtitle: Subtitle
migration.bullet_convert_agents: Converts agent.yaml to agent.toml with proper capabilities
migration.bullet_copy_workspace: Copies workspace files and memory data
migration.bullet_map_tools: Maps tools (read_file → file_read, execute_command → shell_exec, etc.)
migration.desc: Seamlessly transfer your agents, memory, workspace files, and channel configurations from OpenClaw to OpenFang.
migration.not_found_desc: Could not auto-detect an OpenClaw installation.
migration.title: Migrate from OpenClaw
security.defense_in_depth_desc: OpenFang implements 15 layered security features across the entire stack — from network ingress to agent sandboxing to cryptographic audit trails. Core protections cannot be disabled.
settings.budget.form_note: Set to 0 for unlimited/per-agent default. Changes apply immediately (in-memory, not persisted to config.toml).
settings.budget.hourly_token_label: Hourly Token Limit (per agent, 0 = use per-agent values)
settings.budget.subtitle: Monitor and control spending across all agents.
settings.budget.title: Budget & Spending Limits
settings.config_field_default_model_api_: Config Field Default Model Api Key Env
settings.config_field_default_model_base: Config Field Default Model Base Url
settings.config_field_extensions_health_: Config Field Extensions Health Check Interval Secs
settings.tool_desc.a2a_discover: Discover an external A2A agent by fetching its agent card from a URL. Returns the agent's name, description, skills, and supported protocols.
settings.tool_desc.a2a_send: Send a task/message to an external A2A agent and get the response. Use agent_name to send to a previously discovered agent, or agent_url for direct addressing.
settings.tool_desc.agent_find: Discover agents by name, tag, tool, or description. Use to find specialists before delegating work.
settings.tool_desc.agent_kill: Kill (terminate) another agent by its ID.
settings.tool_desc.agent_list: List all currently running agents with their IDs, names, states, and models.
settings.tool_desc.agent_send: Send a message to another agent and receive their response. Accepts UUID or agent name. Use agent_find first to discover agents.
settings.tool_desc.agent_spawn: Spawn a new agent from a TOML manifest. Returns the new agent's ID and name.
settings.tool_desc.apply_patch: Apply a multi-hunk diff patch to add, update, move, or delete files. Use this for targeted edits instead of full file overwrites.
settings.tool_desc.browser_back: Go back to the previous page in browser history.
settings.tool_desc.browser_click: Click an element on the current browser page by CSS selector or visible text. Returns the resulting page state.
settings.tool_desc.browser_close: Close the browser session. The browser will also auto-close when the agent loop ends.
settings.tool_desc.browser_navigate: Navigate a browser to a URL. Returns the page title and readable content as markdown. Opens a persistent browser session.
settings.tool_desc.browser_read_page: Read the current browser page content as structured markdown. Use after clicking or navigating to see the updated page.
settings.tool_desc.browser_run_js: Run JavaScript on the current browser page and return the result. For advanced interactions that other browser tools cannot handle.
settings.tool_desc.browser_screenshot: Take a screenshot of the current browser page. Returns a base64-encoded PNG image.
settings.tool_desc.browser_scroll: Scroll the browser page. Use this to see content below the fold or navigate long pages.
settings.tool_desc.browser_type: Type text into an input field on the current browser page.
settings.tool_desc.browser_wait: Wait for a CSS selector to appear on the page. Useful for dynamic content that loads asynchronously.
settings.tool_desc.canvas_present: Present an interactive HTML canvas to the user. The HTML is sanitized (no scripts, no event handlers) and saved to the workspace. The dashboard will render it in a panel. Use for rich data visualizations, formatted reports, or interactive UI.
settings.tool_desc.channel_send: Send a message or media to a user on a configured channel (email, telegram, slack, etc). For email: recipient is the email address; optionally set subject. For media: set image_url, file_url, or file_path to send an image or file instead of (or alongside) text. Use thread_id to reply in a specific thread/topic.
settings.tool_desc.cron_cancel: Cancel a scheduled/cron job by its ID.
settings.tool_desc.cron_create: Create a scheduled/cron job. Supports one-shot (at), recurring (every N seconds), and cron expressions. Max 50 jobs per agent.
settings.tool_desc.cron_list: List all scheduled/cron jobs for the current agent.
settings.tool_desc.docker_exec: Execute a command inside a Docker container sandbox. Provides OS-level isolation with resource limits, network isolation, and capability dropping. Requires Docker to be installed and docker.enabled=true.
settings.tool_desc.event_publish: Publish a custom event that can trigger proactive agents. Use to broadcast signals to the agent fleet.
settings.tool_desc.file_list: List files in a directory. Paths are relative to the agent workspace.
settings.tool_desc.file_read: Read the contents of a file. Paths are relative to the agent workspace.
settings.tool_desc.file_write: Write content to a file. Paths are relative to the agent workspace.
settings.tool_desc.hand_activate: Activate a Hand — spawns a specialized autonomous agent with curated tools and skills.
settings.tool_desc.hand_deactivate: Deactivate a running Hand and stop its agent.
settings.tool_desc.hand_list: List available Hands (curated autonomous packages) and their activation status.
settings.tool_desc.hand_status: Check the status and metrics of an active Hand.
settings.tool_desc.image_analyze: Analyze an image file — returns format, dimensions, file size, and a base64 preview. For vision-model analysis, include a prompt.
settings.tool_desc.image_generate: Generate images from a text prompt using DALL-E 3, DALL-E 2, or GPT-Image-1. Requires OPENAI_API_KEY. Generated images are saved to the workspace output/ directory.
settings.tool_desc.knowledge_add_entity: Add an entity to the knowledge graph. Entities represent people, organizations, projects, concepts, locations, tools, etc.
settings.tool_desc.knowledge_add_relatio: Add a relation between two entities in the knowledge graph.
settings.tool_desc.knowledge_query: Query the knowledge graph. Filter by source entity, relation type, and/or target entity. Returns matching entity-relation-entity triples.
settings.tool_desc.location_get: Get approximate geographic location based on IP address. Returns city, country, coordinates, and timezone.
settings.tool_desc.media_describe: Describe an image using a vision-capable LLM. Auto-selects the best available provider (Anthropic, OpenAI, or Gemini). Returns a text description of the image content.
settings.tool_desc.media_transcribe: Transcribe audio to text using speech-to-text. Auto-selects the best available provider (Groq Whisper or OpenAI Whisper). Returns the transcript.
settings.tool_desc.memory_recall: Recall a value from shared memory by key.
settings.tool_desc.memory_store: Store a value in shared memory accessible by all agents. Use for cross-agent coordination and data sharing.
settings.tool_desc.process_kill: Terminate a running process and clean up its resources.
settings.tool_desc.process_list: List all running processes for the current agent, including their IDs, commands, uptime, and alive status.
settings.tool_desc.process_poll: Read accumulated stdout/stderr from a running process. Non-blocking: returns whatever output has buffered since the last poll.
settings.tool_desc.process_start: Start a long-running process (REPL, server, watcher). Returns a process_id for subsequent poll/write/kill operations. Max 5 processes per agent.
settings.tool_desc.process_write: Write data to a running process's stdin. A newline is appended automatically if not present.
settings.tool_desc.schedule_create: Schedule a recurring task using natural language or cron syntax. Examples: 'every 5 minutes', 'daily at 9am', 'weekdays at 6pm', '0 */5 * * *'.
settings.tool_desc.schedule_delete: Remove a scheduled task by its ID.
settings.tool_desc.schedule_list: List all scheduled tasks with their IDs, descriptions, schedules, and next run times.
settings.tool_desc.shell_exec: Execute a shell command and return its output.
settings.tool_desc.speech_to_text: Transcribe audio to text using speech-to-text. Auto-selects Groq Whisper or OpenAI Whisper. Supported formats: mp3, wav, ogg, flac, m4a, webm.
settings.tool_desc.system_time: Get the current date, time, and timezone. Returns ISO 8601 timestamp, Unix epoch seconds, and timezone info.
settings.tool_desc.task_claim: Claim the next available task from the task queue assigned to you or unassigned.
settings.tool_desc.task_complete: Mark a previously claimed task as completed with a result.
settings.tool_desc.task_list: List tasks in the shared queue, optionally filtered by status (pending, in_progress, completed).
settings.tool_desc.task_post: Post a task to the shared task queue for another agent to pick up.
settings.tool_desc.text_to_speech: Convert text to speech audio. Auto-selects OpenAI or ElevenLabs. Saves audio to workspace output/ directory.
settings.tool_desc.web_fetch: Fetch a URL with SSRF protection. Supports GET/POST/PUT/PATCH/DELETE. For GET, HTML is converted to Markdown. For other methods, returns raw response body.
settings.tool_desc.web_search: Search the web using multiple providers (Tavily, Brave, Perplexity, DuckDuckGo) with automatic fallback. Returns structured results with titles, URLs, and snippets.
wizard.title: Title
workflows.label: Label
security.core.path_traversal.name: Path Traversal Prevention
security.core.path_traversal.desc: Blocks directory escape attacks (../) in all file operations. Two-phase validation: syntactic rejection of path components, then canonicalization to normalize symlinks.
security.core.path_traversal.threat: Directory escape, privilege escalation via symlinks
security.core.ssrf_protection.name: SSRF Protection
security.core.ssrf_protection.desc: Blocks outbound requests to private IPs, localhost, and cloud metadata endpoints (AWS/GCP/Azure). Validates DNS resolution results to defeat rebinding attacks.
security.core.ssrf_protection.threat: Internal network reconnaissance, cloud credential theft
security.core.capability_system.name: Capability-Based Access Control
security.core.capability_system.desc: Deny-by-default permission system. Every agent operation (file I/O, network, shell, memory, spawn) requires an explicit capability grant in the manifest.
security.core.capability_system.impl: host_functions.rs — check_capability() on every host function
security.core.privilege_escalation_preve: When a parent agent spawns a child, the kernel enforces child capabilities are a subset of parent capabilities. No agent can grant rights it does not have.
Capability escalation through agent spawning chains
security.core.subprocess_isolation.name: Subprocess Environment Isolation
security.core.subprocess_isolation.desc: Child processes (shell tools) inherit only a safe allow-list of environment variables. API keys, database passwords, and secrets are never leaked to subprocesses.
security.core.subprocess_isolation.threa: Secret exfiltration via child process environment
security.core.security_headers.name: Security Headers
security.core.security_headers.desc: Every HTTP response includes CSP, X-Frame-Options: DENY, X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff, Referrer-Policy, and X-XSS-Protection headers.
security.core.security_headers.threat: XSS, clickjacking, MIME sniffing, content injection
security.core.wire_hmac_auth.name: Wire Protocol Authentication
security.core.wire_hmac_auth.desc: Agent-to-agent OFP connections use HMAC-SHA256 mutual authentication with nonce-based handshake and constant-time signature comparison (subtle crate).
security.core.request_id_tracking.name: Request ID Tracking
security.core.request_id_tracking.desc: Every API request receives a unique UUID (x-request-id header) and is logged with method, path, status code, and latency for full traceability.
security.ctrl.rate_limiter.name: API Rate Limiting
security.ctrl.rate_limiter.desc: GCRA (Generic Cell Rate Algorithm) with cost-aware tokens. Different endpoints cost different amounts — spawning an agent costs 50 tokens, health check costs 1.
security.ctrl.rate_limiter.hint: Hard-coded: 500 tokens/minute per IP. Edit rate_limiter.rs to tune.
security.ctrl.websocket_limits.name: WebSocket Connection Limits
security.ctrl.websocket_limits.desc: Per-IP connection cap prevents connection exhaustion. Idle timeout closes abandoned connections. Message rate limiting prevents flooding.
security.ctrl.websocket_limits.hint: Hard-coded: 5 connections/IP, 30min idle timeout, 64KB max message. Edit ws.rs to tune.
security.ctrl.wasm_sandbox.name: WASM Dual Metering
security.ctrl.wasm_sandbox.desc: WASM modules run with two independent resource limits: fuel metering (CPU instruction count) and epoch interruption (wall-clock timeout with watchdog thread).
security.ctrl.wasm_sandbox.hint: Default: 1M fuel units, 30s timeout. Configurable per-agent via SandboxConfig.
security.ctrl.auth.name: Bearer Token Authentication
security.ctrl.auth.desc: All non-health endpoints require Authorization: Bearer header. When no API key is configured, all requests are restricted to localhost only.
security.ctrl.auth.hint: Set api_key in ~/.openfang/config.toml for remote access. Empty = localhost only.
security.mon.audit_trail.name: Merkle Audit Trail
security.mon.audit_trail.desc: Every security-critical action is appended to an immutable, tamper-evident log. Each entry is cryptographically linked to the previous via SHA-256 hash chain.
security.mon.audit_trail.hint: Always active. Verify chain integrity from the Audit Log page.
security.mon.taint_tracking.name: Information Flow Taint Tracking
security.mon.taint_tracking.desc: Labels data by provenance (ExternalNetwork, UserInput, PII, Secret, UntrustedAgent) and blocks unsafe flows: external data cannot reach shell_exec, secrets cannot reach network.
security.mon.taint_tracking.hint: Always active. Prevents data flow attacks automatically.
security.mon.manifest_signing.name: Ed25519 Manifest Signing
security.mon.manifest_signing.desc: Agent manifests can be cryptographically signed with Ed25519. Verify manifest integrity before loading to prevent supply chain tampering.
security.mon.manifest_signing.hint: Available for use. Sign manifests with ed25519-dalek for verification.
security.fmt.websocket_limits: Max {{max}} conn/IP | {{idle_min}} min idle timeout | {{msg_kb}} KB max msg
security.audit_chain_desc: Run cryptographic verification of the entire SHA-256 Merkle hash chain.
